definition: Classification of the day's trading behavior based on early session price action. Determines which trading model to apply.
feeds_into: entry_models (profile type determines which strategy to use)
related_to: power_of_three_and_AMD (each profile corresponds to a PO3 pattern)
bridge_integration: After 10:30 AM ET, classify the day profile. If delayed → only analyze setups during NY Silver Bullet window (10:00–11:00 AM ET). If ranging → reduce max open positions from 3 to 1 and require Grade A confluence minimum. Profile classification overrides default session entry logic.

types.london_normal_buy: London sweeps Asian low (takes SSL) → reverses bullish → NY continues bullish. Classic PO3: Asian=accumulation, London sweep=manipulation, NY=distribution up.
types.london_normal_sell: London sweeps Asian high (takes BSL) → reverses bearish → NY continues bearish. Mirror of buy day.
types.london_delayed_buy: London is indecisive, no clear sweep. Manipulation delayed to NY session. NY sweeps low → rallies. Patience required.
types.london_delayed_sell: London indecisive. NY sweeps high → sells off. Don't force trades during London on these days.
types.ranging: Neither session produces clear manipulation. Low-conviction day. Reduce position sizes or skip.

detection_timing: Profile type can be identified by 10:30 AM ET at the latest. Before that, you're guessing. Do not commit to a model before London has had time to act (2:00-5:00 AM ET) and NY has opened (9:30 AM ET).

london_normal_buy: 1. Asian range established (clear high and low from 8 PM–2 AM ET).
2. London sweeps Asian LOW within first 60 minutes of open (2:00–3:00 AM ET).
3. Displacement candle forms bullish within 15–30 min of the sweep.
4. BOS above Asian range high confirms bullish intent — this is the entry trigger.
london_normal_sell: 2. London sweeps Asian HIGH within first 60 minutes of open.
3. Displacement candle forms bearish within 15–30 min of the sweep.
4. BOS below Asian range low confirms bearish intent — entry trigger.
london_delayed_buy: 1. London session has no clear sweep of Asian levels — price stays inside or barely touches Asian range.
2. Price drifts or chops through London session with no displacement.
3. NY open (9:30 AM ET) sweeps Asian low or London session low → reversal begins.
4. Entry comes in the 9:30–10:30 ET window after reversal displacement confirms.
london_delayed_sell: 1. London session has no clear sweep of Asian levels.
2. Price stays flat or drifts up without displacement.
3. NY open sweeps Asian high or London session high → sells off.
4. Entry 9:30–10:30 ET after bearish displacement confirms.
ranging: 1. CBDR width is greater than 60% of the ADR — pre-market range already consumed most of the day's expected move.
2. London fails to break Asian range in either direction.
3. No displacement candle forms in London or early NY.
Action: Reduce position size by 50% or skip the session entirely.

profile_to_strategy_mapping.london_normal: Standard ICT entry model: OB + FVG after sweep. Enter on retest of displacement FVG. Target is opposite session high/low.
profile_to_strategy_mapping.london_delayed: Wait for NY Silver Bullet window (10:00–11:00 AM ET). Do not force trades during London. Use the same OB+FVG entry but only after NY sweep and reversal displacement.
profile_to_strategy_mapping.ranging: Avoid standard setups. If trading, micro-scalp only within established range. Do not attempt swing entries.

failure_mode: If a London Normal setup triggers but price reverses against the trade after the initial move, this becomes a 'whipsaw day'. The profile mislabeled, or manipulation extended. Do not re-enter in the same direction. Avoid the rest of the session or wait for next kill zone with fresh context.
connection_to_PO3: The profile IS PO3 applied with session-specific timing. 'London Normal' = PO3 on time. 'London Delayed' = manipulation pushed to NY. 'Ranging' = no clear PO3 cycle = don't trade.
